title: leleba vulgaris
side_effects: Leleba vulgaris can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to its potent alkaloid content. Long-term use can also lead to cardiovascular problems, including hypertension and arrhythmias, as well as kidney damage. In some cases, excessive consumption of leleba vulgaris can cause allergic reactions, such as hives, itching, and difficulty breathing. Additionally, using leleba vulgaris for medicinal purposes without proper medical supervision can lead to interactions with other medications, exacerbating existing health conditions.
